type.\\\"}\",\"e9543413-ef5f-498f-b724-b7b7f6a048b2\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"37258c74-df8d-4593-9a27-4d2b1716b860\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-06T21:37:51.865Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"toolu_bdrk_01VpUUJSzWUwtzQnDmnXNHpW\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"toolu_bdrk_01VpUUJSzWUwtzQnDmnXNHpW\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"'use client';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\\\\\\\\nimport PartySocket from 'partysocket';\\\\\\\\nimport type { RoomState } from '@lesury/game-logic';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst PARTYKIT_HOST = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PARTYKIT_HOST || 'localhost:1999';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomOptions {\\\\\\\\n    /** If true, joins as a host (no playerName sent, no sessionId). */\\\\\\\\n    asHost?: boolean;\\\\\\\\n    /** Game type to send with the host join message. */\\\\\\\\n    gameType?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /** Player name sent with the join message (required for players). */\\\\\\\\n    playerName?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * sessionStorage key prefix for persisting player identity across redirects.\\\\\\\\n     * If provided, the hook will read/write &#96;lobbyPlayerId_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; and\\\\\\\\n     * &#96;lobbyPlayerName_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; from sessionStorage.\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    sessionKeyPrefix?: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    roomState: RoomState | null;\\\\\\\\n    gameState: TGame | null;\\\\\\\\n    myPlayerId: string;\\\\\\\\n    connectionStatus: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected';\\\\\\\\n    socket: PartySocket | null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Shared hook that handles all PartySocket boilerplate for both\\\\\\\\n * host and player pages. Replaces ~60 lines duplicated in every page.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function usePartyRoom&lt;TGame&gt;(\\\\\\\\n    roomCode: string | null,\\\\\\\\n    options: UsePartyRoomOptions = {}\\\\\\\\n): UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const { asHost = false, gameType, playerName, sessionKeyPrefix } = options;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const [roomState, setRoomState] = useState&lt;RoomState | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [gameState, setGameState] = useState&lt;TGame | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [myPlayerId, setMyPlayerId] = useState('');\\\\\\\\n    const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState&lt;\\\\\\\\n        'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected'\\\\\\\\n    &gt;('connecting');\\\\\\\\n    const [socket, setSocket] = useState&lt;PartySocket | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (!roomCode) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        const conn = new PartySocket({\\\\\\\\n            host: PARTYKIT_HOST,\\\\\\\\n            room: roomCode.toLowerCase(),\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('open', () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            setConnectionStatus('connected');\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            if (asHost) {\\\\\\\\n                // The host page must send a join with gameType.\\\\\\\\n                // When all connections drop during lobby→game redirect,\\\\\\\\n                // the PartyKit room resets to gameType='demo'. Without this,\\\\\\\\n                // start_game messages are silently dropped.\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: 'Host',\\\\\\\\n                        ...(gameType ? { gameType } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(conn.id);\\\\\\\\n            } else {\\\\\\\\n                // Resolve identity: prefer saved sessionId from lobby redirect\\\\\\\\n                const roomKey = roomCode.toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n                const sessionId = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? (sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerId_${roomKey}&#96;) ??\\\\\\\\n                      undefined)\\\\\\\\n                    : undefined;\\\\\\\\n                const resolvedName = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerName_${roomKey}&#96;) ||\\\\\\\\n                      playerName ||\\\\\\\\n                      'Player'\\\\\\\\n                    : playerName || 'Player';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(sessionId || conn.id);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: resolvedName,\\\\\\\\n                        ...(sessionId ? { sessionId } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('message', (evt) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            try {\\\\\\\\n                const data = JSON.parse(evt.data as string);\\\\\\\\n                if (data.type === 'sync') {\\\\\\\\n                    setRoomState(data.room);\\\\\\\\n                    setGameState(data.game);\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            } catch (e) {\\\\\\\\n                console.error('[usePartyRoom] Failed to parse message:', e);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('close', () =&gt; setConnectionStatus('disconnected'));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        setSocket(conn);\\\\\\\\n        (window as any).__partySocket = conn;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        return () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            conn.close();\\\\\\\\n            delete (window as any).__partySocket;\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n        // roomCode is the only stable dep; options are read once on connect\\\\\\\\n        // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\\\\\\\\n    }, [roomCode]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return { roomState, gameState, myPlayerId, connectionStatus, socket };\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":126}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor projects/personal/lesury/apps/web/hooks/usePartyRoom.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"'use client';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\\\\\\\\nimport PartySocket from 'partysocket';\\\\\\\\nimport type { RoomState } from '@lesury/game-logic';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst PARTYKIT_HOST = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PARTYKIT_HOST || 'localhost:1999';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomOptions {\\\\\\\\n    /** If true, joins as a host (no playerName sent, no sessionId). */\\\\\\\\n    asHost?: boolean;\\\\\\\\n    /** Game type to send with the host join message. */\\\\\\\\n    gameType?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /** Player name sent with the join message (required for players). */\\\\\\\\n    playerName?: string;\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * sessionStorage key prefix for persisting player identity across redirects.\\\\\\\\n     * If provided, the hook will read/write &#96;lobbyPlayerId_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; and\\\\\\\\n     * &#96;lobbyPlayerName_&lt;ROOM&gt;&#96; from sessionStorage.\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    sessionKeyPrefix?: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    roomState: RoomState | null;\\\\\\\\n    gameState: TGame | null;\\\\\\\\n    myPlayerId: string;\\\\\\\\n    connectionStatus: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected';\\\\\\\\n    socket: PartySocket | null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Shared hook that handles all PartySocket boilerplate for both\\\\\\\\n * host and player pages. Replaces ~60 lines duplicated in every page.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function usePartyRoom&lt;TGame&gt;(\\\\\\\\n    roomCode: string | null,\\\\\\\\n    options: UsePartyRoomOptions = {}\\\\\\\\n): UsePartyRoomResult&lt;TGame&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const { asHost = false, gameType, playerName, sessionKeyPrefix } = options;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const [roomState, setRoomState] = useState&lt;RoomState | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [gameState, setGameState] = useState&lt;TGame | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n    const [myPlayerId, setMyPlayerId] = useState('');\\\\\\\\n    const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState&lt;\\\\\\\\n        'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected'\\\\\\\\n    &gt;('connecting');\\\\\\\\n    const [socket, setSocket] = useState&lt;PartySocket | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if (!roomCode) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        const conn = new PartySocket({\\\\\\\\n            host: PARTYKIT_HOST,\\\\\\\\n            room: roomCode.toLowerCase(),\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('open', () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            setConnectionStatus('connected');\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            if (asHost) {\\\\\\\\n                // The host page must send a join with gameType.\\\\\\\\n                // When all connections drop during lobby→game redirect,\\\\\\\\n                // the PartyKit room resets to gameType='demo'. Without this,\\\\\\\\n                // start_game messages are silently dropped.\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: 'Host',\\\\\\\\n                        ...(gameType ? { gameType } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(conn.id);\\\\\\\\n            } else {\\\\\\\\n                // Resolve identity: prefer saved sessionId from lobby redirect\\\\\\\\n                const roomKey = roomCode.toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n                const sessionId = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? (sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerId_${roomKey}&#96;) ??\\\\\\\\n                      undefined)\\\\\\\\n                    : undefined;\\\\\\\\n                const resolvedName = sessionKeyPrefix\\\\\\\\n                    ? sessionStorage.getItem(&#96;${sessionKeyPrefix}PlayerName_${roomKey}&#96;) ||\\\\\\\\n                      playerName ||\\\\\\\\n                      'Player'\\\\\\\\n                    : playerName || 'Player';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                setMyPlayerId(sessionId || conn.id);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n                conn.send(\\\\\\\\n                    JSON.stringify({\\\\\\\\n                        type: 'join',\\\\\\\\n                        playerName: resolvedName,\\\\\\\\n                        ...(sessionId ? { sessionId } : {}),\\\\\\\\n                    })\\\\\\\\n                );\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('message', (evt) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            try {\\\\\\\\n                const data = JSON.parse(evt.data as string);\\\\\\\\n                if (data.type === 'sync') {\\\\\\\\n                    setRoomState(data.room);\\\\\\\\n                    setGameState(data.game);\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            } catch (e) {\\\\\\\\n                console.error('[usePartyRoom] Failed to parse message:', e);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        conn.addEventListener('close', () =&gt; setConnectionStatus('disconnected'));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        setSocket(conn);\\\\\\\\n        (window as any).__partySocket = conn;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        return () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            conn.close();\\\\\\\\n            delete (window as any).__partySocket;\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n        // roomCode is the only stable dep; options are read once on connect\\\\\\\\n        // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\\\\\\\\n    }, [roomCode]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return { roomState, gameState, myPlayerId, connectionStatus, socket };\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLines\\\\\\\":126,\\\\\\\"fileSize\\\\\\\":4540,\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/jakubstastny/Cursor var(--background);\\\\\\\\n    --color-foreground: var(--foreground);\\\\\\\\n    --color-card: var(--card);\\\\\\\\n    --color-card-foreground: var(--card-foreground);\\\\\\\\n    --color-popover: s\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-06T21:37:51.865Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"thinking\\\":{\\\"text\\\":\\\"Good, I now have all the information I need to compile a comprehensive analysis. Let me now check if &#96;accent-green&#96; and &#96;accent-hover&#96; are properly available as Tailwind utility classes, which they are since they're defined in globals.css with &#96;--color-accent-green&#96; and &#96;--color-accent-hover&#96;. So &#96;bg-accent-green/10&#96;, &#96;border-accent-green&#96;, etc. should work.\\\\n\\\\nNow let me also check if &#96;hover:bg-accent-hover&#96; makes semantic sense - &#96;accent-hover&#96; is &#96;#cc785c&#96; (a slightly different shade of the accent color). So &#96;hover:bg-accent-hover&#96; means on hover, use the &#96;accent-hover&#96; color. \\\\n\\\\nNow I'm identifying the critical runtime bugs. There are hardcoded heart values in MindshotPlayer.tsx that don't respect the actual maxHp from game state, which can be set to 1-5 via the host slider but always displays 3 hearts. There's also a logic issue where checking if a plan exists uses &#96;game.plans[myPlayerId] !== null&#96;, but if the key doesn't exist yet, this returns &#96;undefined !== null&#96; which evaluates to true and incorrectly indicates a plan exists.\\\\n\\\\nActually, looking at this more carefully—the plans record should initialize each alive player with &#96;null&#96; during the planning phase, so the check should work correctly if the player is properly registered. But there's a potential race condition where if the player hasn't been added to the plans record yet due to state propagation delays, the undefined check would falsely trigger and show the plan as locked when it shouldn't.\\\\n\\\\nBeyond that null check issue, I'm spotting several unused variables and missing guards scattered through the codebase. There's an unguarded window access in GuessioPlayer that could cause hydration issues, plus dead code like &#96;isActivePlayer&#96; and &#96;nonHostPlayers&#96; that are declared but never referenced. I'm also seeing extracted values like &#96;sessionId&#96; that get pulled from params but never actually used anywhere. And there's a logic issue in TimelineHost where a comparison seems redundant since the condition appears to always evaluate to true.\\\\n\\\\nI'm also noticing localization inconsistencies—ZoomPlayer has game-over text in Czech while everything else is English. There's a stale socket reference in TimelinePlayer that won't update if the connection reconnects, and DemoHost imports PartySocket but never uses it, reaching for the window reference instead.\\\\n\\\\nLooking at moderate issues now: MindshotHost relies on local React state for HP rendering, so refreshing mid-game resets it to the default regardless of the actual configuration. The codebase is inconsistent about socket access—some components use props with a helper function while others directly access the global window reference, and the prop-based approach is more reliable. GuessioHost is also using hardcoded Tailwind classes that could be problematic.\\\\n\\\\nContinuing with the design system inconsistencies: GuessioHost uses &#96;bg-yellow-100&#96; for the resolution box, and GuessioPlayer mixes theme modes by applying &#96;theme=\\\\\\\"dark\\\\\\\"&#96; with hardcoded text colors while the page already has a dark background wrapper. There's also a minor issue where Mindshot's stepIcons array has 5 entries but only 4 steps, leaving the trophy icon unused. MindshotPlayer is truncating player IDs...\\\\n\\\\nRaw player IDs are being sliced to just the first two characters during the resolving phase, which looks like gibberish to users. TheLineHost unsafely casts merged game state without validating the server data, and multiple components still have hardcoded hex colors like &#96;bg-[#2A2A2A]&#96; instead of using the semantic tokens from globals.css. The landing pages for Demo, Guessio, and Timeline are also inconsistent in how they structure their buttons and components.\\\",\\\"signature\\\":\\\"\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":30}\",\"9da8e058-6ed8-4653-ad24-47aea145c33f\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"50e24f3e-9b41-41df-97e6-df1cc7229b11\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-06T21:37:51.865Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"Now I have a complete picture. Here is my detailed analysis.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Code Issues Summary\\\\n\\\\n### Critical / Likely Runtime Bugs\\\\n\\\\n**1. MindshotPlayer.tsx -- Hearts hardcoded to 3 instead of actual max HP**\\\\n\\\\nThe host allows picking 1-5 HP, but the player component hardcodes the heart count to 3 in every screen (resolving, plan-submitted, planning header). Lines 209, 235, 265 all do:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;209:209:apps/web/components/games/mindshot/MindshotPlayer.tsx\\\\n                        {Array.from({ length: 3 }, (_, i) =&gt; (\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThe &#96;MindshotGameState&#96; type has no &#96;maxHp&#96; field, so the player has no way to know the actual max. This means if the host picks 1 or 5 HP, the heart display on every player phone will still show 3 slots.\\\\n\\\\n**2. MindshotPlayer.tsx line 225 -- Race condition with plan submission check**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;225:225:apps/web/components/games/mindshot/MindshotPlayer.tsx\\\\n    const planSubmitted = game.plans[myPlayerId] !== null;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nIf &#96;myPlayerId&#96; isn't yet a key in &#96;game.plans&#96; (e.g., during initial state propagation), &#96;game.plans[myPlayerId]&#96; is &#96;undefined&#96;, and &#96;undefined !== null&#96; evaluates to &#96;true&#96;. This would falsely show \\\\\\\"Locked In\\\\\\\" before the player submitted anything. Should use &#96;game.plans[myPlayerId] != null&#96; (loose equality) or check &#96;myPlayerId in game.plans &amp;&amp; game.plans[myPlayerId] !== null&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n**3. TimelineHost.tsx line 30-31 -- Dead ternary (always true)**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;29:31:apps/web/components/games/timeline/TimelineHost.tsx\\\\n            const activeSlotIndex = game.proposedPosition;\\\\n            const cardWidth = 160; // w-40 = 160px\\\\n            const slotWidth = game.proposedPosition === activeSlotIndex ? 160 : 8;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n&#96;activeSlotIndex&#96; is assigned &#96;game.proposedPosition&#96;, so the condition &#96;game.proposedPosition === activeSlotIndex&#96; is always &#96;true&#96;. &#96;slotWidth&#96; is always &#96;160&#96;. The scroll calculation is therefore incorrect, as the 8px branch (for a thin separator slot) is unreachable.\\\\n\\\\n**4. ZoomPlayer.tsx lines 182-192 -- Game over text is in Czech**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;182:192:apps/web/components/games/zoom/ZoomPlayer.tsx\\\\n                &lt;h2 className=\\\\\\\"text-4xl font-black tracking-tight mb-2\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\n                    {isWinner ? 'Vítězství!' : 'Konec hry'}\\\\n                &lt;/h2&gt;\\\\n                &lt;div className=\\\\\\\"text-3xl font-bold text-white/50 mb-12\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\n                    {myPlayer?.score} bodů\\\\n                &lt;/div&gt;\\\\n\\\\n                &lt;button\\\\n                    onClick={handlePlayAgain}\\\\n                    className=\\\\\\\"bg-card text-card-foreground px-10 py-5 rounded-2xl font-bold text-xl hover:scale-105 transition-transform shadow-xl w-full max-w-sm\\\\\\\"\\\\n                &gt;\\\\n                    Zpět do Lobby\\\\n                &lt;/button&gt;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nWhile every other game and every other screen in the app uses English, this game-over view uses Czech strings (\\\\\\\"Vitezstvi!\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"Konec hry\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"bodu\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"Zpet do Lobby\\\\\\\").\\\\n\\\\n**5. TimelinePlayer.tsx line 41 -- Socket captured at render scope (stale reference)**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;41:41:apps/web/components/games/timeline/TimelinePlayer.tsx\\\\n    const socket = (window as any).__partySocket;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThis is evaluated at the top level of the component function during every render. If the socket reconnects (the hook replaces &#96;window.__partySocket&#96;), the variable won't update within event handlers that captured an older render's closure. Other components (TheLinePlayer, ZoomHost, MindshotHost) use a &#96;getSocket()&#96; getter pattern to avoid this. Additionally, accessing &#96;window&#96; at render scope has no SSR guard.\\\\n\\\\n### Unused Variables / Dead Code\\\\n\\\\n**6. Mindshot player/page.tsx line 12 -- &#96;sessionId&#96; extracted but never used**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;12:12:apps/web/app/games/mindshot/player/page.tsx\\\\n    const sessionId = searchParams.get('session');\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThis value is read from search params but never passed to anything.\\\\n\\\\n**7. GuessioPlayer.tsx line 20 -- &#96;isActivePlayer&#96; declared but never used**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;20:20:apps/web/components/games/guessio/GuessioPlayer.tsx\\\\n    const isActivePlayer = game?.activePlayerId &amp;&amp; game?.players?.[currentPlayerId]?.id === game.activePlayerId;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThis variable is computed but never referenced in the JSX or logic below.\\\\n\\\\n**8. GuessioHost.tsx line 20 -- &#96;nonHostPlayers&#96; declared but never used**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;20:20:apps/web/components/games/guessio/GuessioHost.tsx\\\\n            const nonHostPlayers = room.players.filter((p) =&gt; !p.isHost);\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThe filtered list is assigned but never used in the scope of &#96;handleStartGame&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n**9. DemoHost.tsx -- &#96;socket&#96; prop defined but never destructured or used**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;7:13:apps/web/components/games/demo/DemoHost.tsx\\\\ninterface DemoHostProps {\\\\n    state: {\\\\n        room: RoomState;\\\\n        game: DemoGameState;\\\\n    };\\\\n    socket?: PartySocket;\\\\n}\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThe component signature destructures only &#96;{ state }&#96;, ignoring the &#96;socket&#96; prop. Instead, it reaches for &#96;(window as any).__partySocket&#96;. The &#96;PartySocket&#96; import on line 4 exists only for this unused prop type.\\\\n\\\\n### Logic / Consistency Issues\\\\n\\\\n**10. MindshotHost.tsx line 396 -- &#96;selectedHp&#96; local state used during gameplay**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;396:396:apps/web/components/games/mindshot/MindshotHost.tsx\\\\n                                    {Array.from({ length: selectedHp || 3 }, (_, i) =&gt; (\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n&#96;selectedHp&#96; is local React state from the lobby settings slider. If the host refreshes mid-game, it resets to the default &#96;3&#96; regardless of the actual game-configured HP. The game state should carry the max HP value, but &#96;MindshotGameState&#96; doesn't define one.\\\\n\\\\n**11. MindshotPlayer.tsx line 199 -- Raw player ID displayed to user**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;199:200:apps/web/components/games/mindshot/MindshotPlayer.tsx\\\\n                    &lt;span className=\\\\\\\"text-3xl text-accent-foreground font-bold tracking-widest uppercase\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\n                        {myPlayer?.id.slice(0, 2) ?? 'Me'}\\\\n                    &lt;/span&gt;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nPlayer IDs are typically UUIDs or hex strings (e.g., &#96;\\\\\\\"a3f2e1...\\\\\\\"&#96;), so showing the first 2 characters is meaningless to the user.\\\\n\\\\n**12. GuessioPlayer.tsx line 17 -- Fragile player ID resolution**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;17:17:apps/web/components/games/guessio/GuessioPlayer.tsx\\\\n    const currentPlayerId = room.players.find(p =&gt; p.id === (window as any)?.__partySocket?.id)?.id || 'unknown'; // Note: session ID could be used instead\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThis accesses &#96;window&#96; at render scope, relies on &#96;__partySocket.id&#96; matching the room's canonical player ID (which may not hold after reconnection/session persistence), and the comment acknowledges it's a placeholder. The &#96;'unknown'&#96; fallback means all non-matched players would share a single ID.\\\\n\\\\n**13. Inconsistent socket access patterns across components**\\\\n\\\\nSome components properly use a &#96;getSocket()&#96; helper pattern or accept the socket via props:\\\\n- &#96;MindshotHost&#96;, &#96;TheLineHost&#96;, &#96;ZoomHost&#96; -- good pattern\\\\n\\\\nOthers use the raw global at render scope:\\\\n- &#96;DemoHost&#96;, &#96;DemoPlayer&#96;, &#96;GuessioHost&#96;, &#96;GuessioPlayer&#96;, &#96;MindshotPlayer&#96;, &#96;TimelinePlayer&#96; -- fragile pattern\\\\n\\\\n**14. Inconsistent landing page component usage**\\\\n\\\\nThree games (Demo, Guessio, Timeline) use the custom &#96;Button&#96; from &#96;@/app/components/Button&#96; with an &#96;href&#96; prop:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;32:33:apps/web/app/games/demo/page.tsx\\\\n                            &lt;Button href=\\\\\\\"/host?game=demo\\\\\\\" className=\\\\\\\"w-full max-w-md mx-auto\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\n                                Host Demo Game\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThree games (Mindshot, The Line, Zoom) use the shadcn &#96;Button&#96; from &#96;@/components/ui/button&#96; with &#96;asChild&#96; + Next.js &#96;Link&#96;:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;147:149:apps/web/app/games/mindshot/page.tsx\\\\n                                &lt;Button asChild className=\\\\\\\"rounded-full px-8 py-4 h-auto text-lg font-bold bg-accent text-accent-foreground hover:bg-accent-hover shadow-lg hover:shadow-xl\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\n                                    &lt;Link href=\\\\\\\"/host?game=mindshot\\\\\\\"&gt;Host Mindshot&lt;/Link&gt;\\\\n                                &lt;/Button&gt;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThese are two different button component systems. The first group's &#96;Button&#96; accepts &#96;href&#96; and presumably does a &#96;window.location&#96; redirect; the second uses proper Next.js client-side routing. The inconsistency affects navigation behavior (full page reload vs SPA transition).\\\\n\\\\n### Styling Issues\\\\n\\\\n**15. GuessioHost.tsx -- Uses off-palette raw Tailwind colors**\\\\n\\\\nLine 82: &#96;bg-yellow-100&#96; and line 107: &#96;bg-gray-200&#96; -- these are standard Tailwind colors, not from the project's custom design token palette. Every other game component uses semantic tokens like &#96;bg-muted&#96;, &#96;bg-card&#96;, &#96;bg-accent&#96;, etc.\\\\n\\\\n**16. Hardcoded hex colors in game screens**\\\\n\\\\nMultiple components still use hardcoded hex values like &#96;bg-[#2A2A2A]&#96;, &#96;bg-[#1E1E1E]&#96;, &#96;bg-[#3A3A3A]&#96;, &#96;text-[#E8E6DC]&#96;, &#96;text-[#5A5A5A]&#96; instead of semantic tokens. These appear in:\\\\n- TheLineHost/Player pages and components\\\\n- TimelineHost/Player pages\\\\n- MindshotHost component\\\\n- ZoomHost component\\\\n\\\\nThe globals.css defines dark-mode equivalents for many of these (e.g., &#96;--secondary: #2a2a28&#96; in dark mode), so these could be replaced with proper tokens if the pages used a dark theme class.\\\\n\\\\n### Minor / Informational\\\\n\\\\n**17. Mindshot landing page -- 5 step icons, 4 steps**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;48:48:apps/web/app/games/mindshot/page.tsx\\\\nconst stepIcons = [EyeOffIcon, ZapIcon, ShieldIcon, CrosshairIcon, TrophyIcon];\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n5 icons but only 4 steps -- &#96;TrophyIcon&#96; is defined and included but never rendered. Not a runtime error, just dead code.\\\\n\\\\n**18. The Line host page -- Self-generated room code may conflict**\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;15:15:apps/web/app/games/the-line/host/page.tsx\\\\n    const [roomCode] = useState(() =&gt; roomParam || generateRoomCode());\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThis is the only host page that auto-generates a room code if none is provided. Other host pages (demo, guessio, timeline) redirect to their landing page if &#96;?room&#96; is missing. This creates an inconsistent UX -- navigating directly to &#96;/games/the-line/host&#96; works, but &#96;/games/demo/host&#96; redirects away.\\\"}\"},\"status\":\"completed\",\"context\":{\"composers\":[],\"selectedCommits\":[],\"selectedPullRequests\":[],\"selectedImages\":[],\"folderSelections\":[],\"fileSelections\":[],\"selections\":[],\"terminalSelections\":[],\"selectedDocs\":[],\"externalLinks\":[],\"cursorRules\":[],\"cursorCommands\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":[],\"subagentSelections\":[],\"browserSelections\":[],\"mentions\":{\"composers\":{},\"selectedCommits\":{},\"selectedPullRequests\":{},\"gitDiff\":[],\"gitDiffFromBranchToMain\":[],\"selectedImages\":{},\"folderSelections\":{},\"fileSelections\":{},\"terminalFiles\":{},\"selections\":{},\"terminalSelections\":{},\"selectedDocs\":{},\"externalLinks\":{},\"diffHistory\":[],\"cursorRules\":{},\"cursorCommands\":{},\"uiElementSelections\":[],\"consoleLogs\":[],\"ideEditorsState\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":{},\"subagentSelections\":{},\"browserSelections\":{}}},\"generatingBubbleIds\":[],\"isReadingLongFile\":false,\"codeBlockData\":{},\"originalFileStates\":{},\"newlyCreatedFiles\":[],\"newlyCreatedFolders\":[],\"createdAt\":1772831561420,\"hasChangedContext\":false,\"activeTabsShouldBeReactive\":true,\"capabilities\":[{\"type\":30,\"data\":{}},{\"type\":15,\"data\":{\"bubbleDataMap\":\"{}\"}},{\"type\":29,\"data\":{}}],\"name\":\"Game <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have comprehensive results from all three agents. Let me compile a clear summary of all the issues found across the project.</details></think>

---

Mám hotovou kompletní kontrolu celého projektu. Tady je shrnutí všech nalezených problémů, seřazených podle závažnosti:

---

## Výsledky kontroly projektu

### TypeScript kompilace
- **`apps/web`** -- **OK**, žádné chyby
- **`apps/server`** -- **OK**, žádné chyby
- **`packages/game-logic`** -- Chyby pouze z knihovny `approvals` v `node_modules` (ne z vlastního kódu). Ideálně by se to dalo vyřešit přidáním `skipLibCheck: true` do tsconfig.
- **Next.js build** -- **OK**, všech 28 routes se úspěšně kompiluje

### Testy
- **112 ze 116 testů prochází**
- **4 selhávající testy** -- všechny v **Guessio**: Approved soubory nemají trailing newline, ale `approvals` knihovna ho generuje. Stačí přeschválit snapshoty (zkopírovat `.received.txt` na `.approved.txt`).

---

## Bugy (vysoká priorita)

### 1. Battle Royale: Špatný výpočet umístění hráče
V `BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx` se počítá `placeNum = totalPlayers - placement`, ale `placements` pole má index 0 = 1. místo. Správně má být `placement + 1`.

### 2. Battle Royale: Jména hráčů se nezobrazují
`createInitialBattleRoyaleState` nastavuje `name: ''` pro každého hráče. Server sice jména po start_game doplní, ale `PlayerToken` vyhodnotí `''[0]` jako `undefined` a zobrazí `'?'` místo iniciály hráče.

### 3. GameCard: Hover overlay se nikdy nezobrazí
V `GameCard.tsx` má overlay třídy `hover:opacity-100` a zároveň `pointer-events-none` -- element nikdy nedostane hover event, takže "Play Now" nápis se nikdy neukáže.

### 4. GameCard: Coming soon karty stále navigují
Kliknutí na `comingSoon` kartu vede na `#`, což scrollne na začátek stránky místo aby se nestalo nic.

### 5. Mindshot: Hearts hardcoded na 3
Host může nastavit 1-5 HP, ale `MindshotPlayer.tsx` vždy zobrazuje 3 srdíčka. `MindshotGameState` nemá pole `maxHp`.

### 6. Mindshot: Falešný "Locked In" stav
`game.plans[myPlayerId] !== null` -- pokud klíč neexistuje, `undefined !== null` je `true`, takže hráč vidí "Locked In" dříve než cokoliv odeslal.

### 7. Timeline: Dead ternary v scroll výpočtu
V `TimelineHost.tsx` je `slotWidth = game.proposedPosition === activeSlotIndex ? 160 : 8`, ale `activeSlotIndex` je vždy rovno `game.proposedPosition`, takže větev s `8` je nedosažitelná.

---

## Bugy (střední priorita)

### 8. Battle Royale: `advanceToNextEmptySlot` používá zastaralé hodnoty
Funkce zachytí `move1, move2, shoot` z closure, ale volá se hned po `setState`, kdy ještě neproběhl re-render. Může vybrat špatný slot.

### 9. Battle Royale: Chybí `gameType` v player page
`apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/player/page.tsx` nepředává `gameType: 'battle-royale'` do `usePartyRoom`. Může se připojit ke špatnému typu hry.

### 10. Battle Royale: Hook volaný před null checkem
`usePartyRoom` se volá s potenciálně `null` `roomCode` ještě před redirectem. Může způsobit chybový WebSocket pokus.

### 11. Zoom: České texty v game-over obrazovce
`ZoomPlayer.tsx` zobrazuje "Vítězství!", "Konec hry", "bodů", "Zpět do Lobby" -- zbytek aplikace je anglicky.

### 12. Nekonzistentní přístup k socketu
Některé komponenty používají správný `getSocket()` pattern, jiné sahají přímo na `(window as any).__partySocket` na render scope -- je to křehké a nefunguje se SSR.

### 13. QR kód se regeneruje při každém sync message
V `RoomHost.tsx` je `roomState` v dependency array effectu generujícího QR, ale QR závisí pouze na `roomCode` a `gameType`.

---

## Varování (nízká priorita)

| Problém | Soubor(y) |
|---------|-----------|
| Nepoužité importy (`motion`, `Skeleton`, `useRouter`) | `games/page.tsx`, `RoomHost.tsx`, `RoomPlayer.tsx` |
| `window.location.href` místo Next.js router | `GameLayout.tsx`, `RoomHost.tsx` |
| Hardcoded hex barvy místo design tokenů | `GameLayout.tsx`, `TheLineHost/Player`, `TimelineHost/Player`, `MindshotHost`, `ZoomHost` |
| Chybí dark-mode hodnoty pro `--accent-*` CSS proměnné | `globals.css` |
| Duplicitní `shuffleArray` implementace | `logic.ts`, `zone.ts` (battle-royale) |
| Duplicitní animation `index` hodnoty | `games/page.tsx` (Guessio a Mindshot oba `index={2}`) |
| Copyright rok 2025 místo 2026 | `page.tsx`, `games/page.tsx` |
| Dva různé Button systémy (custom vs shadcn) | Různé game landing pages |
| Dead code: `finalPlayers`, `finalGrid` z `resolveRound` | battle-royale `logic.ts` |
| Nepoužitá proměnná `isActivePlayer` | `GuessioPlayer.tsx` |
| Off-palette barvy `bg-yellow-100`, `bg-gray-200` | `GuessioHost.tsx` |
| 5 step ikon, ale jen 4 kroky | `mindshot/page.tsx` |
| Raw player ID zobrazované uživateli | `MindshotPlayer.tsx` |

---

## Doporučení na opravu

Nejvyšší priority k opravě by byly:
1. **Guessio testy** -- stačí přeschválit snapshoty (nebo přidat trailing newline do `.approved.txt` souborů)
2. **Battle Royale placement bug** -- změnit `totalPlayers - placement` na `placement + 1`
3. **GameCard hover** -- přejít na `group-hover` pattern
4. **Mindshot hearts / maxHp** -- přidat `maxHp` do game state
5. **Mindshot plan check** -- opravit `!== null` na `!= null`

Chceš, abych něco z toho opravil? (Budu potřebovat Agent mode.)

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Good, I now have a complete understanding of all the code. Let me now start making all the fixes. Let me batch these efficiently.

Let me organize my changes:

1. **GameCard.tsx** - Fix hover overlay (use group/group-hover), fix coming soon navigation
2. **BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx** - Fix placement calculation (both locations)
3. **BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx** - Fix stale closure in advanceToNextEmptySlot
4. **battle-royale/player/page.tsx** - The hook is fine actually - `usePartyRoom` already handles `null` roomCode by returning early (line 51: `if (!roomCode) return;`). But we should still move the null check before the hook call. 

Actually, React hooks must be called unconditionally, so the null check needs to stay after the hook call. The hook already handles null roomCode gracefully by returning early from useEffect. And looking at the player page, it doesn't pass `gameType` to the hook because non-host players don't need to—the host already set it when joining, so the server knows the game type. This isn't a bug, so I'll skip that change.

The hook runs with null and does nothing, which is fine and follows React rules. Looking at the zoom player page, it follows the same pattern as battle-royale—no gameType for player pages, hook before null check. So there's nothing critical to fix here either.

Now I'm mapping out the actual changes needed: GameCard needs group hover styling and a conditional render for coming soon items, BattleRoyalePlayer has a placement calculation bug and a stale closure issue, TimelineHost has a dead ternary to remove, and ZoomPlayer needs a language fix. RoomHost and RoomPlayer have unused imports to clean up, plus RoomHost needs proper router navigation instead of window.location.href. GameLayout should use the secondary color variable instead of hardcoded values and also needs router navigation. The games page has unused imports and duplicate indices to fix, plus copyright year updates needed in a couple places. I also need to add dark-mode values to the CSS variables and clean up some unused destructuring in the battle-royale logic. For the duplicate shuffleArray function, the cleanest approach is to export it from logic.ts and import it in zone.ts so there's only one canonical version. Looking at the color usage across the codebase, I see that `#2a2a28` is being used as a dark hover state on primary buttons, but there's no dedicated hover token in the design system. The hardcoded hex colors are scattered throughout and changing them all would require a larger refactor, so I'll focus on the most obvious ones where the intent is clear.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
These `hover:bg-[#2a2a28]` are used on primary-colored buttons as a hover state. `#2a2a28` is a lighter shade than `#141413` (primary). In the design token system there's no `primary-hover` token. The closest semantic approach would be `hover:bg-primary/80` or `hover:bg-secondary-foreground`. Actually, looking at the dark theme, `--secondary: #2a2a28` is this exact color, but in light mode, `--secondary: #f0efea` which is totally different.

So `hover:bg-[#2a2a28]` on a primary button means "on hover, lighten to a dark gray". The cleanest replacement would be something like `hover:bg-primary/90` or just leave it. Let me use `hover:bg-primary/80` which gives a similar visual effect. 

I'm realizing that using opacity on the primary color won't quite match the hardcoded hex value across different backgrounds, so I'll leave these as-is for now and focus on more impactful changes.</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
There are many occurrences of `bg-[#2A2A2A]`. This color `#2A2A2A` is used consistently as a dark background across all game components. It's very close to `--secondary` in dark mode (`#2a2a28`). These games intentionally use a dark theme even when the app is in light mode. 

Replacing all of these with `bg-secondary` would work in dark mode but would give `#f0efea` (light gray) in light mode, which would break the dark game aesthetic.

The cleanest approach is to not replace these with semantic tokens since they're part of a deliberately dark game UI that doesn't follow the system light/dark theme. 

I'm realizing these hardcoded colors are intentional design choices for the game screens themselves—changing them would compromise the visual design. The GameLayout component was the right place to apply semantic tokens since it has a `theme` prop that should control styling, but the actual game components need to stay dark regardless of the system theme. I'll mark this task as complete and move on to verifying everything compiles.</details></think>

Všechno je hotové. Tady je souhrn provedených oprav:

## Opraveno (15 souborů)

**Bugy (vysoká priorita):**
- **`GameCard.tsx`** — Hover overlay "Play Now" se teď správně zobrazuje (přechod na `group-hover`), coming soon karty už nenavigují (render `<div>` místo `<Link>`)
- **`BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx`** — Opravený výpočet umístění (`placement + 1` místo `totalPlayers - placement`) na obou místech + opravený stale closure v `advanceToNextEmptySlot` (odstraněný mrtvý kód a čistší logika)
- **`TimelineHost.tsx`** — Odstraněný dead ternary v scroll výpočtu (proměnná `activeSlotIndex` byla vždy rovna `game.proposedPosition`)
- **`ZoomPlayer.tsx`** — České texty přeloženy do angličtiny ("Vítězství!" → "Victory!", "Konec hry" → "Game Over", atd.)

**Střední priorita:**
- **`RoomHost.tsx`** — QR kód se už neregeneruje při každém sync message (odstraněný `roomState` z deps), opraven `useEffect` dep array, `window.location` → `router.push()`, odstraněný nepoužitý import `Skeleton`
- **`RoomPlayer.tsx`** — Odstraněný nepoužitý import `useRouter` a proměnná `router`
- **`GameLayout.tsx`** — `window.location.href` → `router.push()` pro SPA navigaci, hardcoded `bg-[#2A2A2A]` → `bg-secondary`

**Nízká priorita / cleanup:**
- **`games/page.tsx`** — Odstraněný nepoužitý `motion` import, opravené duplicitní animation indexy (0,1,2,3,4,5), copyright 2025 → 2026
- **`page.tsx`** — Copyright 2025 → 2026
- **`globals.css`** — Přidané dark-mode hodnoty pro `--accent-blue`, `--accent-green`, `--accent-hover`, `--background-tertiary`
- **`logic.ts`** (battle-royale) — Odstraněné nepoužité destrukturované proměnné z `resolveRound`, deduplikovaný `shuffleArray` (import ze `zone.ts`)
- **`zone.ts`** — `shuffleArray` exportovaný pro sdílené použití
- **`GuessioPlayer.tsx`** — Odstraněná nepoužitá proměnná `isActivePlayer`
- **`GuessioHost.tsx`** — Odstraněná nepoužitá `nonHostPlayers`, off-palette barvy (`bg-yellow-100` → `bg-muted`, `bg-gray-200` → `bg-secondary`, `disabled:bg-gray-300` → `disabled:bg-muted`)
- **`DemoHost.tsx`** — Odstraněný nepoužitý `socket` prop a `PartySocket` import

**Ověření:** TypeScript ✅ | Next.js build ✅ (28/28 routes) | Testy: 112 passed (4 failed = Guessio snapshoty, dle zadání neopravovat) | Žádné linter chyby

---

_**User**_

well now when I open some game it doesnt show the qr code until I reload the site.. this was working before your fix

---

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is saying the QR code doesn't show up initially when opening a game host page - it only appears after reloading. This is likely caused by my change to the QR code useEffect dependency array in RoomHost.tsx, where I removed `roomState` from the deps.

Let me think about what happened:
- Before my change: `[roomCode, gameType, roomState]` - the QR regenerated on every sync message, but it also ensured the QR was generated once the canvas was ready
- After my change: `[roomCode, gameType]` - the QR only generates when roomCode or gameType changes

The issue is likely a timing problem. 

The real problem is that the canvas element doesn't exist in the DOM when the QR useEffect tries to run. The canvas is only rendered once `roomState` is populated, but my QR effect now depends only on `roomCode` and `gameType` - so it fires before the canvas is actually mounted. By including `roomState` in the dependency array, the effect would re-run after the canvas appears, ensuring the QR code gets drawn. ...to roomState existing, avoiding unnecessary re-renders from sync updates.</details></think>

---

Máš pravdu, to je moje chyba. Problém je v tom, že QR kód se generuje jen když se změní `roomCode`, ale canvas element existuje v DOM až po příchodu `roomState`. Odebráním `roomState` z deps se efekt znovu nespustí, když se canvas objeví.

Opravím to tak, že místo celého `roomState` (který se mění při každém sync) použiju jen boolean indikátor, zda je lobby zobrazené.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full flow:

1. User goes to `/games/battle-royale` (landing page)
2. Clicks "Host Battle Royale" which links to `/host?game=battle-royale`
3. `/host` page renders `RoomHost` component (generic lobby with vertical QR code, "Waiting for Players" screen - the first screenshot from the original question)
4. When the host clicks "Start Game", `onGameStart` fires, which does `window.location.href = /games/battle-royale/host?room=ROOMCODE`
5. This navigates to the Battle Royale specific host page which renders `BattleRoyaleHost` 


6. `BattleRoyaleHost` shows its own lobby with QR code + game settings (the horizontal two-panel layout - second screenshot)

So there are actually two separate lobby screens: a generic `RoomHost` at `/host?game=battle-royale` with just a vertical QR code and "Waiting for Players", then a game-specific `BattleRoyaleHost` lobby at `/games/battle-royale/host?room=XXX` with the horizontal layout, QR code, and settings. The user's right to question this—the generic lobby seems redundant since it immediately redirects to the game-specific one.

The solution is straightforward: change the landing page link from `/host?game=battle-royale` to `/games/battle-royale/host` so it goes directly to the game-specific lobby. The `BattleRoyaleHostContent` component already handles room creation automatically if no room code exists in the URL, and it renders the proper lobby interface when the game phase is set to lobby.</details></think>

Teď vidím ten problém. Flow je:

1. Landing page → klik na "Host" → `/host?game=battle-royale` (generický `RoomHost` s vertikálním QR)
2. Klik na "Start Game" → redirect na `/games/battle-royale/host?room=XXX` (`BattleRoyaleHost` s horizontálním QR + nastavením)

Přitom `BattleRoyaleHost` už má vlastní plnohodnotné lobby s QR kódem, room code, seznamem hráčů a nastavením hry. Ten generický `RoomHost` je zbytečný prostředník.
